an1.170:1.1: "One person, *bhikkhus*, arises in the world for the benefit of many, for the happiness of many, out of compassion for the world, for the good, the benefit, and the happiness of gods and humans.
an1.170:1.3: The Tathāgata, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one.
an1.170:1.4: This is the one person, *bhikkhus*, who arises in the world for the benefit of many, for the happiness of many, out of compassion for the world, for the good, the benefit, and the happiness of gods and humans."
an1.171:1.1: "The appearance of one person, *bhikkhus*, is rare in the world.
an1.171:1.3: Of the Tathāgata, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one.
an1.171:1.4: The appearance of this one person, *bhikkhus*, is rare in the world."
an1.172:1.1: "One person, *bhikkhus*, arises in the world as an extraordinary human being.
an1.172:1.3: The Tathāgata, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one.
an1.172:1.4: This is the one person, *bhikkhus*, who arises in the world as an extraordinary human being."
an1.173:1.1: "The passing away of one person, *bhikkhus*, is a cause of grief for many people.
an1.173:1.3: Of the Tathāgata, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one.
an1.173:1.4: The passing away of this one person, *bhikkhus*, is a cause of grief for many people."
an1.174:1.1: "One person, *bhikkhus*, arises in the world without a second, without a companion, without a counterpart, without an equal, without a match, without a peer, incomparable, unrivalled, the foremost among two-footed beings.
an1.174:1.3: The Tathāgata, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one.
an1.174:1.4: This is the one person, *bhikkhus*, who arises in the world without a second, without a companion, without a counterpart, without an equal, without a match, without a peer, incomparable, unrivalled, the foremost among two-footed beings."
an1.175-186:1.1: "With the appearance of one person, *bhikkhus*, there comes the appearance of great vision, the appearance of great light, the appearance of great radiance, the appearance of the six unsurpassed things, the realization of the four analytical knowledges, the penetration of many elements, the penetration of various elements, the realization of the fruit of knowledge and liberation, the realization of the fruit of stream-entry, the realization of the fruit of once-returning, the realization of the fruit of non-returning, the realization of the fruit of arahantship.
an1.175-186:1.3: Of the Tathāgata, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one.
an1.175-186:1.4: With the appearance of this one person, *bhikkhus*, there comes the appearance of great vision, the appearance of great light, the appearance of great radiance, the appearance of the six unsurpassed things, the realization of the four analytical knowledges, the penetration of many elements, the penetration of various elements, the realization of the fruit of knowledge and liberation, the realization of the fruit of stream-entry, the realization of the fruit of once-returning, the realization of the fruit of non-returning, the realization of the fruit of arahantship."
an1.187:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single person who rightly keeps rolling the unsurpassed wheel of *dhamma* set in motion by the Tathāgata as Sāriputta does.
an1.187:1.2: Sāriputta, *bhikkhus*, rightly keeps rolling the unsurpassed wheel of *dhamma* set in motion by the Tathāgata."
an1.187:1.3: The One Person Chapter, the thirteenth.
